input.headline: Sali Hughes on beauty: from nail polish to powder, the best new makeup of 2025

raw.headline: Sali Hughes on beauty: from nail polish to powder, the best new makeup of 2025

winner: Why did the makeup artist break up with their foundation? It just wasn’t *sali*-brating enough!

candidates.text: I tried the 2025 makeup line—it came with a usage warning: "May cause temporary increase in self-esteem." I’m still figuring out if that’s a feature or a bug.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on a product warning, specific to makeup, concise and funny with a clever play on self-esteem."}

candidates.text: Why did the makeup artist break up with their foundation? It just wasn’t *sali*-brating enough!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "Puns on 'sali-brating' are clever and specific to the headline, but the joke is slightly forced and lacks broader humor impact."}

candidates.text: Why did I stop using that 2025 makeup? Because every time I put on the powder, my skin said, "No, thank you.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses a specific product (powder), and introduces a humorous personification of the skin. However, it lacks some surprise and could be more tightly tied to the headline's context."}

candidates.text: Why did the makeup artist break up with his girlfriend? Because she said he had *too much* shade in his life.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat related to makeup (shade) but lacks specificity to the headline's focus on Sali Hughes and 2025 makeup trends. The pun is mild and not strongly tied to the context."}

candidates.text: Sali Hughes says the hottest new makeup trend in 2025 is 'invisible'—because it’s so bad, you don’t even realize you’re wearing it.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific to the context, but the humor relies on a negative twist that may not align with the positive tone of the headline. It's concise but lacks the punchiness to fully satisfy the funniness criterion."}
